problem: The digits of a two-digit number $AB$ are reversed to form a second two-digit number, and the lesser of the two-digit numbers is subtracted from the greater. What prime number must be a factor of the difference if $A\neq B$?

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why we are reversing the digits of the two-digit number $AB$ to form another two-digit number?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What happens when we subtract the lesser two-digit number (i.e., $BA$) from the greater two-digit number (i.e., $AB$)?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we express the subtraction of $BA$ from $AB$ using mathematical notation?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: By simplifying the expression $AB - BA$, can you identify any common factors?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why is it important to consider the condition $A\neq B$ when determining the prime factor of the difference?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How does the pattern $AB - BA = 3(3\cdot A - 3\cdot B)$ help us determine the prime factor of the difference?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why 3 must be a factor of $AB - BA$ if $A\neq B$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are there any other prime factors that must be considered if $A\neq B$? Why or why not?
classified_questions.label: b
